mapping.a_element: classical string configuration in AdS space with boundary conditions determined by gluon momenta
mapping.b_element: perturbative QCD splitting functions computed via Feynman diagrams and renormalization group evolution

mapping.a_element: gauge/string duality (AdS/CFT) as the computational framework
mapping.b_element: perturbative expansion in QCD with renormalization group methods

mapping.a_element: infrared divergences regulated via dimensional regularization in gravity dual
mapping.b_element: infrared/ultraviolet divergences handled via dimensional regularization in QCD

mapping.a_element: functions of the coupling characterizing IR divergences at strong coupling
mapping.b_element: splitting functions as functions of the coupling in perturbative QCD

brief_justification: While both papers address quantities dependent on coupling and involve dimensional regularization for divergences, their core mechanisms differ fundamentally: Paper A uses a non-perturbative holographic duality (AdS/CFT) to compute scattering amplitudes via classical string theory, whereas Paper B employs perturbative QCD with Feynman diagrams and renormalization group evolution to derive splitting functions. The underlying mathematical structures and physical principles are distinct.
